title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement approved; only 350 authors opted out
excerpt: Anthropic blocks authors from opting out of $1.5B settlement at last minute.
content_html:
Happy ending for Anthropic

Anthropic blocks authors from opting out of $1.5B settlement at last minute.

On Monday, a judge approved a $1.5 billion settlement between Anthropic and authors, ending the largest copyright class-action ever certified and granting the largest copyright settlement ever reached.

Back in May, some authors fought to block the settlement, which was proposed after the court ruled that Anthropic training AI on books was fair use; however, its piracy of works was likely not.

Authors opposing the settlement argued that lawyers’ fees were too high and authors’ payouts were too low. Hoping to avoid accepting the estimated $3,000-per-work payout and file separate lawsuits to seek higher damages, a handful of authors tried to opt out past the deadline.

In her order, US District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín overruled objections to the settlement as lacking merit. She emphasized that about 95 percent of the class received notifications and approximately 91 percent of authors and publishers impacted have already filed claims. Only 350 class members opted out, while another 54 sought to object or filed late opt-out requests, Martínez-Olguín said.

The high rate of participation suggested that most authors received timely notice of the distribution plan, agreed it was “fair,” and supported the settlement, Martínez-Olguín wrote. She also noted that the $3,000-per-work payouts were “four times the minimum statutory damages.”

Lawyers fees, plaintiffs awards cut

Although Martínez-Olguín approved the total settlement amount, she reduced both lawyers’ fees and the requested awards for the three authors who represented the class in the litigation.

Lawyers originally asked for 20 percent of the settlement in fees, requesting $300 million. Ahead of Martínez-Olguín’s ruling, that percentage was reduced to 12.5, with the total fees requested amounting to approximately $187 million.

The judge ruled that 12.5 percent was too high, however, cutting fees down to less than 7 percent of the settlement fund, which is about $101 million.

Some of those fees include future work that lawyers will do to distribute funds, and some authors argued that lawyers’ projections for time spent were alarmingly inflated. Acknowledging these concerns, Martínez-Olguín wrote in a footnote that the court decided to take “an additional step to protect the interests of the Class.” Once payouts have been finalized, lawyers will be required to file a post-distribution accounting, and if their fees are less, then the court “may reduce” them, Martínez-Olguín said.

Although the settlement likely relieves the three authors who spent years defending their works and 506,194 works from potential class members, the lead plaintiffs are likely disappointed by a decision to reduce their requested service awards from $50,000 to $15,000.

But Martínez-Olguín said that the higher amount was “unreasonable,” despite authors spending substantial time and resources on both litigation and the settlement negotiation. Without a sign that authors may face retaliation for filing the lawsuit, the judge ruled that lower awards were due.

In a statement to Reuters, lead plaintiffs said that the settlement “brings us one step closer to real accountability for Anthropic and puts all AI companies on notice they can’t shortcut the law or override creators’ rights.”

It’s unlikely, but still possible, that authors may receive more than $3,000 per work.

“If any funds remain in the Settlement Fund after all Valid Claims are paid, the Parties anticipate a redistribution of the remaining funds to Settlement Class Members unless it is economically infeasible to do so,” Martínez-Olguín said.

Authors’ lawyers celebrated the judge’s approval, writing in a press release that “rightsholders came together to call foul” on Anthropic’s piracy and describing support for the settlement among them as “overwhelming.”

Anthropic also seems happy to be done arguing about the settlement. In a statement provided to Ars, Anthropic’s deputy general counsel, Aparna Sridhar, said that Anthropic is glad the case’s landmark ruling established that its AI training was fair use and is happy that most rightsholders have already filed claims.

“We are pleased that more than 91 percent of authors and publishers covered by the settlement have claimed their share of the payment, and we’re looking forward to bringing this matter to a close,” Sridhar said.

Anthropic blocked last-minute opt-outs

For every opt-out granted, Anthropic risks facing separate litigation on what Martínez-Olguín said remains a complex case for courts to assess. It makes sense then that Anthropic was motivated to block delayed requests to opt out.

Some authors who opposed the settlement argued that they did not receive timely notices, the opt-out period was too short, and the settlement awards to authors were unacceptable, because the Copyright Act allowed for potentially higher statutory damages.

Martínez-Olguín confirmed that 350 class members successfully opted out of the settlement, but dozens of objections were denied, as were at least nine authors’ direct requests to opt out after the March 30 deadline.

Ultimately, Martínez-Olguín overruled most of the objections and denied most of the late opt-out requests.

The court only accepted two late opt-out requests from authors who showed “excusable neglect” in failing to meet the deadline, Martínez-Olguín said.

The two were co-authors who did not receive settlement notices. One author filed an opt-out request a few days late, which was granted, while the other request was granted after a much longer delay, due to the author suffering a stroke. That author also garnered the court’s sympathy by noting that she “lives in Mexico, speaks Spanish, was not able to understand the class notice, and asserts no Spanish translation was provided.”

One of the last authors to try to escape the settlement at the last minute was Donald Passman, an entertainment lawyer and author who sent his request at the end of June. That was three months after the deadline, which Martínez-Olguín said was “inexcusably late” since records showed the notices were delivered to his current address, where at least one notice was “received,” albeit more than a month after the opt-out deadline.

In a court filing, Passman argued that evidence showed he received late notice of the settlement. He allegedly couldn’t even rely on his publisher, Simon & Schuster, to forward notices and “had no information about the existence of any opt-out deadline” until he received a letter past the deadline that only invited him to claim his payout and did not mention opt-outs.

Passman did not immediately respond to Ars’ request to comment on the settlement approval. But he’s likely disappointed, since he argued that his book, All You Need to Know About the Music Business, is a key industry text and “the estimated per-work settlement payment of approximately $3,351.39 does not adequately compensate me for the potential value of my individual copyright claims.”

Anthropic fought Passman’s attempt to opt out, arguing that he took months to file his opt-out request after learning about the settlement. According to Anthropic, it allegedly strained credulity that Passman “somehow did not hear about the largest copyright settlement ever in a case that generated mountains of headlines.”

“Allowing Mr. Passman to opt out after such a long and unjustified delay would invite others to try the same, undermining finality and risking undue delay in the distribution of Settlement funds,” Anthropic argued. “In a Class of hundreds of thousands, only a tiny fraction—approximately 400—have sought to opt out.”

Martínez-Olguín agreed with Anthropic and said that granting Passman’s request risked opening the floodgates for other authors to back out of the settlement.

In her order, Martínez-Olguín reminded authors that the settlement provided non-monetary benefits they seemed to be ignoring. For one, authors can avoid costly, lengthy, complex litigation that could result in a loss with “zero recovery,” she said, citing precedent showing that “prompt closure” provides value to class members. For another, the settlement requires Anthropic to destroy all their works and allows for future litigation, should Anthropic misuse their works after the dust settles.

Ashley is a senior policy reporter for Ars Technica, dedicated to tracking social impacts of emerging policies and new technologies. She is a Chicago-based journalist with 20 years of experience.

54 Comments

blocks.html_en: Happy ending for Anthropic

sentences.en: Happy ending for Anthropic
sentences.zh: 人类的幸福结局

blocks.html_en: Anthropic blocks authors from opting out of $1.5B settlement at last minute.

sentences.en: Anthropic blocks authors from opting out of $1.5B settlement at last minute.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 阻止作者在最后一刻选择退出 $1.5B 和解。

blocks.html_en: On Monday, a judge approved a $1.5 billion settlement between Anthropic and authors, ending the largest copyright class-action ever certified and granting the largest copyright settlement ever reached.

sentences.en: On Monday, a judge approved a $1.5 billion settlement between Anthropic and authors, ending the largest copyright class-action ever certified and granting the largest copyright settlement ever reached.
sentences.zh: 周一，法官批准了 Anthropic 与作者之间达成的 15 亿美元和解协议，结束了有史以来最大的版权集体诉讼，并达成了有史以来最大的版权和解协议。

blocks.html_en: Back in May, some authors fought to block the settlement, which was proposed after the court ruled that Anthropic training AI on books was fair use; however, its piracy of works was likely not.

sentences.en: Back in May, some authors fought to block the settlement, which was proposed after the court ruled that Anthropic training AI on books was fair use; however, its piracy of works was likely not.
sentences.zh: 早在 5 月份，一些作者就曾奋力阻止和解协议，该和解协议是在法院裁定书籍上的人择训练人工智能属于合理使用之后提出的。然而，其盗版作品很可能并非如此。

blocks.html_en: Authors opposing the settlement argued that lawyers’ fees were too high and authors’ payouts were too low. Hoping to avoid accepting the estimated $3,000-per-work payout and file separate lawsuits to seek higher damages, a handful of authors tried to opt out past the deadline.

sentences.en: Authors opposing the settlement argued that lawyers’ fees were too high and authors’ payouts were too low.
sentences.zh: 反对和解的作者认为律师费太高而作者的报酬太低。

sentences.en: Hoping to avoid accepting the estimated $3,000-per-work payout and file separate lawsuits to seek higher damages, a handful of authors tried to opt out past the deadline.
sentences.zh: 为了避免接受每件作品约 3,000 美元的报酬，并单独提起诉讼以寻求更高的赔偿，一些作者试图在截止日期后选择退出。

blocks.html_en: In her order, US District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín overruled objections to the settlement as lacking merit. She emphasized that about 95 percent of the class received notifications and approximately 91 percent of authors and publishers impacted have already filed claims. Only 350 class members opted out, while another 54 sought to object or filed late opt-out requests, Martínez-Olguín said.

sentences.en: In her order, US District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín overruled objections to the settlement as lacking merit.
sentences.zh: 美国地区法官阿拉塞利·马丁内斯-奥尔金在她的命令中驳回了对和解协议的反对意见，认为该和解协议缺乏依据。

sentences.en: She emphasized that about 95 percent of the class received notifications and approximately 91 percent of authors and publishers impacted have already filed claims.
sentences.zh: 她强调，大约 95% 的学生收到了通知，大约 91% 受影响的作者和出版商已经提出索赔。

sentences.en: Only 350 class members opted out, while another 54 sought to object or filed late opt-out requests, Martínez-Olguín said.
sentences.zh: Martínez-Olguín 表示，只有 350 名集体成员选择退出，另有 54 名成员试图反对或延迟提出退出请求。

blocks.html_en: The high rate of participation suggested that most authors received timely notice of the distribution plan, agreed it was “fair,” and supported the settlement, Martínez-Olguín wrote. She also noted that the $3,000-per-work payouts were “four times the minimum statutory damages.”

sentences.en: The high rate of participation suggested that most authors received timely notice of the distribution plan, agreed it was “fair,” and supported the settlement, Martínez-Olguín wrote.
sentences.zh: 马丁内斯-奥尔金写道，高参与率表明大多数作者及时收到了分配计划的通知，认为这是“公平的”，并支持和解。

sentences.en: She also noted that the $3,000-per-work payouts were “four times the minimum statutory damages.”
sentences.zh: 她还指出，每项工作 3,000 美元的赔偿金“是最低法定赔偿金的四倍”。

blocks.html_en: Lawyers fees, plaintiffs awards cut

sentences.zh: 律师费、原告赔偿金削减

blocks.html_en: Although Martínez-Olguín approved the total settlement amount, she reduced both lawyers’ fees and the requested awards for the three authors who represented the class in the litigation.

sentences.zh: 尽管马丁内斯-奥尔金批准了总和解金额，但她减少了律师费以及在诉讼中代表集体的三位作者要求的赔偿金。

sentences.zh: 律师最初要求支付和解费用的 20%，即 3 亿美元。

sentences.en: Ahead of Martínez-Olguín’s ruling, that percentage was reduced to 12.5, with the total fees requested amounting to approximately $187 million.
sentences.zh: 在 Martínez-Olguín 做出裁决之前，这一比例已降至 12.5，要求的费用总额约为 1.87 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: The judge ruled that 12.5 percent was too high, however, cutting fees down to less than 7 percent of the settlement fund, which is about $101 million.

sentences.en: The judge ruled that 12.5 percent was too high, however, cutting fees down to less than 7 percent of the settlement fund, which is about $101 million.
sentences.zh: 然而，法官裁定 12.5% 过高，因此将费用削减至和解基金（约 1.01 亿美元）的 7% 以下。

blocks.html_en: Some of those fees include future work that lawyers will do to distribute funds, and some authors argued that lawyers’ projections for time spent were alarmingly inflated. Acknowledging these concerns, Martínez-Olguín wrote in a footnote that the court decided to take “an additional step to protect the interests of the Class.” Once payouts have been finalized, lawyers will be required to file a post-distribution accounting, and if their fees are less, then the court “may reduce” them, Martínez-Olguín said.

sentences.en: Some of those fees include future work that lawyers will do to distribute funds, and some authors argued that lawyers’ projections for time spent were alarmingly inflated.
sentences.zh: 其中一些费用包括律师未来为分配资金所做的工作，一些作者认为律师对所花费时间的预测被夸大得惊人。

sentences.en: Acknowledging these concerns, Martínez-Olguín wrote in a footnote that the court decided to take “an additional step to protect the interests of the Class.”
sentences.zh: 马丁内斯·奥尔金承认这些担忧，他在脚注中写道，法院决定采取“额外措施来保护集体利益”。

sentences.en: Once payouts have been finalized, lawyers will be required to file a post-distribution accounting, and if their fees are less, then the court “may reduce” them, Martínez-Olguín said.
sentences.zh: 马丁内斯-奥尔金表示，一旦支出最终确定，律师将被要求提交分配后会计报告，如果他们的费用减少，那么法院“可能会减少”费用。

blocks.html_en: Although the settlement likely relieves the three authors who spent years defending their works and 506,194 works from potential class members, the lead plaintiffs are likely disappointed by a decision to reduce their requested service awards from $50,000 to $15,000.

sentences.en: Although the settlement likely relieves the three authors who spent years defending their works and 506,194 works from potential class members, the lead plaintiffs are likely disappointed by a decision to reduce their requested service awards from $50,000 to $15,000.
sentences.zh: 尽管和解可能让三位作者松了一口气，他们花了数年时间捍卫自己的作品和潜在集体成员的 506,194 件作品，但主要原告可能会对将他们请求的服务奖从 50,000 美元减少到 15,000 美元的决定感到失望。

blocks.html_en: But Martínez-Olguín said that the higher amount was “unreasonable,” despite authors spending substantial time and resources on both litigation and the settlement negotiation. Without a sign that authors may face retaliation for filing the lawsuit, the judge ruled that lower awards were due.

sentences.en: But Martínez-Olguín said that the higher amount was “unreasonable,” despite authors spending substantial time and resources on both litigation and the settlement negotiation.
sentences.zh: 但马丁内斯-奥尔金表示，尽管作者在诉讼和和解谈判上花费了大量时间和资源，但较高的金额“不合理”。

sentences.en: Without a sign that authors may face retaliation for filing the lawsuit, the judge ruled that lower awards were due.
sentences.zh: 由于没有迹象表明作者可能因提起诉讼而面临报复，法官裁定应给予较低的赔偿。

blocks.html_en: In a statement to Reuters, lead plaintiffs said that the settlement “brings us one step closer to real accountability for Anthropic and puts all AI companies on notice they can’t shortcut the law or override creators’ rights.”

sentences.en: In a statement to Reuters, lead plaintiffs said that the settlement “brings us one step closer to real accountability for Anthropic and puts all AI companies on notice they can’t shortcut the law or override creators’ rights.”
sentences.zh: 主要原告在给路透社的一份声明中表示，和解协议“让我们离 Anthropic 的真正责任又近了一步，并让所有人工智能公司注意到，他们不能逃避法律或凌驾于创作者的权利之上。”

blocks.html_en: It’s unlikely, but still possible, that authors may receive more than $3,000 per work.

sentences.en: It’s unlikely, but still possible, that authors may receive more than $3,000 per work.
sentences.zh: 作者每件作品获得超过 3,000 美元的收入虽然不太可能，但仍有可能。

blocks.html_en: “If any funds remain in the Settlement Fund after all Valid Claims are paid, the Parties anticipate a redistribution of the remaining funds to Settlement Class Members unless it is economically infeasible to do so,” Martínez-Olguín said.

sentences.en: “If any funds remain in the Settlement Fund after all Valid Claims are paid, the Parties anticipate a redistribution of the remaining funds to Settlement Class Members unless it is economically infeasible to do so,” Martínez-Olguín said.
sentences.zh: “如果在支付所有有效索赔后和解基金中仍有任何资金，双方预计会将剩余资金重新分配给和解集体成员，除非这样做在经济上不可行，”马丁内斯-奥尔金说。

blocks.html_en: Authors’ lawyers celebrated the judge’s approval, writing in a press release that “rightsholders came together to call foul” on Anthropic’s piracy and describing support for the settlement among them as “overwhelming.”

sentences.en: Authors’ lawyers celebrated the judge’s approval, writing in a press release that “rightsholders came together to call foul” on Anthropic’s piracy and describing support for the settlement among them as “overwhelming.”
sentences.zh: 作者的律师对法官的批准表示庆祝，并在一份新闻稿中写道，“版权持有者聚集在一起对 Anthropic 的盗版行为表示不满”，并称他们对和解的支持“压倒性”。

blocks.html_en: Anthropic also seems happy to be done arguing about the settlement. In a statement provided to Ars, Anthropic’s deputy general counsel, Aparna Sridhar, said that Anthropic is glad the case’s landmark ruling established that its AI training was fair use and is happy that most rightsholders have already filed claims.

sentences.en: Anthropic also seems happy to be done arguing about the settlement.
sentences.zh: 人类似乎也很高兴结束了关于和解的争论。

sentences.en: In a statement provided to Ars, Anthropic’s deputy general counsel, Aparna Sridhar, said that Anthropic is glad the case’s landmark ruling established that its AI training was fair use and is happy that most rightsholders have already filed claims.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 的副总法律顾问 Aparna Sridhar 在向 Ars 提供的一份声明中表示，Anthropic 很高兴该案件具有里程碑意义的裁决证明其人工智能培训属于合理使用，并且很高兴大多数权利持有人已经提出索赔。

blocks.html_en: “We are pleased that more than 91 percent of authors and publishers covered by the settlement have claimed their share of the payment, and we’re looking forward to bringing this matter to a close,” Sridhar said.

sentences.en: “We are pleased that more than 91 percent of authors and publishers covered by the settlement have claimed their share of the payment, and we’re looking forward to bringing this matter to a close,” Sridhar said.
sentences.zh: 斯里达尔表示：“我们很高兴和解协议涵盖的超过 91% 的作者和出版商都要求支付他们应得的份额，我们期待着解决这个问题。”

blocks.html_en: Anthropic blocked last-minute opt-outs

sentences.en: Anthropic blocked last-minute opt-outs
sentences.zh: 人类阻止了最后一刻的选择退出

blocks.html_en: For every opt-out granted, Anthropic risks facing separate litigation on what Martínez-Olguín said remains a complex case for courts to assess. It makes sense then that Anthropic was motivated to block delayed requests to opt out.

sentences.en: For every opt-out granted, Anthropic risks facing separate litigation on what Martínez-Olguín said remains a complex case for courts to assess.
sentences.zh: 对于每一次批准的选择退出，Anthropic 都面临着单独诉讼的风险，马丁内斯-奥尔金表示，这仍然是一个需要法院评估的复杂案件。

sentences.en: It makes sense then that Anthropic was motivated to block delayed requests to opt out.
sentences.zh: 那么，Anthropic 的动机是阻止延迟的选择退出请求，这是有道理的。

blocks.html_en: Some authors who opposed the settlement argued that they did not receive timely notices, the opt-out period was too short, and the settlement awards to authors were unacceptable, because the Copyright Act allowed for potentially higher statutory damages.

sentences.en: Some authors who opposed the settlement argued that they did not receive timely notices, the opt-out period was too short, and the settlement awards to authors were unacceptable, because the Copyright Act allowed for potentially higher statutory damages.
sentences.zh: 一些反对和解的作者辩称，他们没有及时收到通知，选择退出期限太短，而且对作者的和解裁决是不可接受的，因为《版权法》允许潜在的更高的法定损害赔偿。

blocks.html_en: Martínez-Olguín confirmed that 350 class members successfully opted out of the settlement, but dozens of objections were denied, as were at least nine authors’ direct requests to opt out after the March 30 deadline.

sentences.en: Martínez-Olguín confirmed that 350 class members successfully opted out of the settlement, but dozens of objections were denied, as were at least nine authors’ direct requests to opt out after the March 30 deadline.
sentences.zh: Martínez-Olguín 证实，有 350 名集体成员成功选择退出和解，但数十项反对意见被驳回，至少 9 位作者在 3 月 30 日截止日期后直接提出选择退出的请求也被拒绝。

blocks.html_en: Ultimately, Martínez-Olguín overruled most of the objections and denied most of the late opt-out requests.

sentences.en: Ultimately, Martínez-Olguín overruled most of the objections and denied most of the late opt-out requests.
sentences.zh: 最终，马丁内斯-奥尔金驳回了大部分反对意见，并拒绝了大部分迟来的选择退出请求。

blocks.html_en: The court only accepted two late opt-out requests from authors who showed “excusable neglect” in failing to meet the deadline, Martínez-Olguín said.

sentences.en: The court only accepted two late opt-out requests from authors who showed “excusable neglect” in failing to meet the deadline, Martínez-Olguín said.
sentences.zh: 马丁内斯-奥尔金说，法院只接受了两位作者提出的迟到的选择退出请求，这些作者因未能在截止日期前完成而表现出“可原谅的疏忽”。

blocks.html_en: The two were co-authors who did not receive settlement notices. One author filed an opt-out request a few days late, which was granted, while the other request was granted after a much longer delay, due to the author suffering a stroke. That author also garnered the court’s sympathy by noting that she “lives in Mexico, speaks Spanish, was not able to understand the class notice, and asserts no Spanish translation was provided.”

sentences.en: The two were co-authors who did not receive settlement notices.
sentences.zh: 两人是共同作者，但没有收到和解通知。

sentences.en: One author filed an opt-out request a few days late, which was granted, while the other request was granted after a much longer delay, due to the author suffering a stroke.
sentences.zh: 一位作者晚几天提出了选择退出请求，该请求获得批准，而另一位作者由于中风，在更长时间的延迟后才获得批准。

sentences.en: That author also garnered the court’s sympathy by noting that she “lives in Mexico, speaks Spanish, was not able to understand the class notice, and asserts no Spanish translation was provided.”
sentences.zh: 该作者还指出，她“住在墨西哥，说西班牙语，无法理解集体通知，并声称没有提供西班牙语翻译”，从而赢得了法院的同情。

blocks.html_en: One of the last authors to try to escape the settlement at the last minute was Donald Passman, an entertainment lawyer and author who sent his request at the end of June. That was three months after the deadline, which Martínez-Olguín said was “inexcusably late” since records showed the notices were delivered to his current address, where at least one notice was “received,” albeit more than a month after the opt-out deadline.

sentences.en: One of the last authors to try to escape the settlement at the last minute was Donald Passman, an entertainment lawyer and author who sent his request at the end of June.
sentences.zh: 最后一刻试图逃避和解的作者之一是唐纳德·帕斯曼 (Donald Passman)，他是一位娱乐律师兼作家，他于 6 月底发出了请求。

sentences.en: That was three months after the deadline, which Martínez-Olguín said was “inexcusably late” since records showed the notices were delivered to his current address, where at least one notice was “received,” albeit more than a month after the opt-out deadline.
sentences.zh: 那是在截止日期之后三个月，马丁内斯-奥尔金说这是“不可原谅的迟到”，因为记录显示通知已发送到他目前的地址，并且至少“收到了一份通知”，尽管比选择退出截止日期晚了一个多月。

blocks.html_en: In a court filing, Passman argued that evidence showed he received late notice of the settlement. He allegedly couldn’t even rely on his publisher, Simon & Schuster, to forward notices and “had no information about the existence of any opt-out deadline” until he received a letter past the deadline that only invited him to claim his payout and did not mention opt-outs.

sentences.en: In a court filing, Passman argued that evidence showed he received late notice of the settlement.
sentences.zh: 帕斯曼在一份法庭文件中辩称，证据显示他收到了较晚的和解通知。

sentences.en: He allegedly couldn’t even rely on his publisher, Simon & Schuster, to forward notices and “had no information about the existence of any opt-out deadline” until he received a letter past the deadline that only invited him to claim his payout and did not mention opt-outs.
sentences.zh: 据称，他甚至无法依赖出版商西蒙与舒斯特（Simon & Schuster）转发通知，并且“不知道是否存在任何选择退出的截止日期”，直到他在截止日期之后收到一封信，只邀请他领取付款，而没有提及选择退出。

blocks.html_en: Passman did not immediately respond to Ars’ request to comment on the settlement approval. But he’s likely disappointed, since he argued that his book, All You Need to Know About the Music Business, is a key industry text and “the estimated per-work settlement payment of approximately $3,351.39 does not adequately compensate me for the potential value of my individual copyright claims.”

sentences.en: Passman did not immediately respond to Ars’ request to comment on the settlement approval.
sentences.zh: 帕斯曼没有立即回应 Ars 就和解批准发表评论的请求。

sentences.en: But he’s likely disappointed, since he argued that his book, All You Need to Know About the Music Business, is a key industry text and “the estimated per-work settlement payment of approximately $3,351.39 does not adequately compensate me for the potential value of my individual copyright claims.”
sentences.zh: 但他可能会感到失望，因为他认为他的书《关于音乐行业你需要知道的一切》是一本重要的行业文本，“每件作品的和解费用预计约为 3,351.39 美元，不足以补偿我个人版权索赔的潜在价值。”

blocks.html_en: Anthropic fought Passman’s attempt to opt out, arguing that he took months to file his opt-out request after learning about the settlement. According to Anthropic, it allegedly strained credulity that Passman “somehow did not hear about the largest copyright settlement ever in a case that generated mountains of headlines.”

sentences.en: Anthropic fought Passman’s attempt to opt out, arguing that he took months to file his opt-out request after learning about the settlement.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 反对帕斯曼选择退出的企图，称他在得知和解协议后花了几个月的时间才提出退出请求。

sentences.en: According to Anthropic, it allegedly strained credulity that Passman “somehow did not hear about the largest copyright settlement ever in a case that generated mountains of headlines.”
sentences.zh: 据 Anthropic 称，据称帕斯曼“不知何故没有听说在这起引起大量头条新闻的案件中达成有史以来最大的版权和解协议”，这令人难以置信。

blocks.html_en: “Allowing Mr. Passman to opt out after such a long and unjustified delay would invite others to try the same, undermining finality and risking undue delay in the distribution of Settlement funds,” Anthropic argued. “In a Class of hundreds of thousands, only a tiny fraction—approximately 400—have sought to opt out.”

sentences.en: “Allowing Mr. Passman to opt out after such a long and unjustified delay would invite others to try the same, undermining finality and risking undue delay in the distribution of Settlement funds,” Anthropic argued.
sentences.zh: “在如此长时间且不合理的拖延之后，允许帕斯曼先生选择退出，将导致其他人也做出同样的尝试，从而破坏最终结果，并冒着和解资金分配不当延迟的风险，”Anthropic 辩称。

sentences.en: “In a Class of hundreds of thousands, only a tiny fraction—approximately 400—have sought to opt out.”
sentences.zh: “在数十万人的班级中，只有一小部分（大约 400 人）试图选择退出。”

blocks.html_en: Martínez-Olguín agreed with Anthropic and said that granting Passman’s request risked opening the floodgates for other authors to back out of the settlement.

sentences.en: Martínez-Olguín agreed with Anthropic and said that granting Passman’s request risked opening the floodgates for other authors to back out of the settlement.
sentences.zh: 马丁内斯-奥尔金同意 Anthropic 的观点，并表示同意帕斯曼的请求可能会导致其他作者退出和解。

blocks.html_en: In her order, Martínez-Olguín reminded authors that the settlement provided non-monetary benefits they seemed to be ignoring. For one, authors can avoid costly, lengthy, complex litigation that could result in a loss with “zero recovery,” she said, citing precedent showing that “prompt closure” provides value to class members. For another, the settlement requires Anthropic to destroy all their works and allows for future litigation, should Anthropic misuse their works after the dust settles.

sentences.en: In her order, Martínez-Olguín reminded authors that the settlement provided non-monetary benefits they seemed to be ignoring.
sentences.zh: 马丁内斯·奥尔金在命令中提醒作者，和解协议提供了他们似乎忽视的非金钱利益。

sentences.en: For one, authors can avoid costly, lengthy, complex litigation that could result in a loss with “zero recovery,” she said, citing precedent showing that “prompt closure” provides value to class members.
sentences.zh: 首先，作者可以避免昂贵、漫长、复杂的诉讼，这些诉讼可能导致“零赔偿”的损失，她说，并援引先例表明“迅速结案”为集体成员提供了价值。

sentences.en: For another, the settlement requires Anthropic to destroy all their works and allows for future litigation, should Anthropic misuse their works after the dust settles.
sentences.zh: 另一方面，和解协议要求 Anthropic 销毁他们的所有作品，并允许在尘埃落定后，如果 Anthropic 滥用他们的作品，可以在未来提起诉讼。

blocks.html_en: Ashley is a senior policy reporter for Ars Technica, dedicated to tracking social impacts of emerging policies and new technologies. She is a Chicago-based journalist with 20 years of experience.

sentences.en: Ashley is a senior policy reporter for Ars Technica, dedicated to tracking social impacts of emerging policies and new technologies.
sentences.zh: 阿什利 (Ashley) 是 Ars Technica 的高级政策记者，致力于追踪新兴政策和新技术的社会影响。

sentences.en: She is a Chicago-based journalist with 20 years of experience.
sentences.zh: 她是一位拥有 20 年经验的芝加哥记者。

blocks.html_en: 54 Comments

sentences.en: 54 Comments
sentences.zh: 54 条评论

content_html_zh:
人类的幸福结局

Anthropic 阻止作者在最后一刻选择退出 $1.5B 和解。

周一，法官批准了 Anthropic 与作者之间达成的 15 亿美元和解协议，结束了有史以来最大的版权集体诉讼，并达成了有史以来最大的版权和解协议。

早在 5 月份，一些作者就曾奋力阻止和解协议，该和解协议是在法院裁定书籍上的人择训练人工智能属于合理使用之后提出的。然而，其盗版作品很可能并非如此。

反对和解的作者认为律师费太高而作者的报酬太低。 为了避免接受每件作品约 3,000 美元的报酬，并单独提起诉讼以寻求更高的赔偿，一些作者试图在截止日期后选择退出。

美国地区法官阿拉塞利·马丁内斯-奥尔金在她的命令中驳回了对和解协议的反对意见，认为该和解协议缺乏依据。 她强调，大约 95% 的学生收到了通知，大约 91% 受影响的作者和出版商已经提出索赔。 Martínez-Olguín 表示，只有 350 名集体成员选择退出，另有 54 名成员试图反对或延迟提出退出请求。

马丁内斯-奥尔金写道，高参与率表明大多数作者及时收到了分配计划的通知，认为这是“公平的”，并支持和解。 她还指出，每项工作 3,000 美元的赔偿金“是最低法定赔偿金的四倍”。

律师费、原告赔偿金削减

尽管马丁内斯-奥尔金批准了总和解金额，但她减少了律师费以及在诉讼中代表集体的三位作者要求的赔偿金。

律师最初要求支付和解费用的 20%，即 3 亿美元。 在 Martínez-Olguín 做出裁决之前，这一比例已降至 12.5，要求的费用总额约为 1.87 亿美元。

然而，法官裁定 12.5% 过高，因此将费用削减至和解基金（约 1.01 亿美元）的 7% 以下。

其中一些费用包括律师未来为分配资金所做的工作，一些作者认为律师对所花费时间的预测被夸大得惊人。 马丁内斯·奥尔金承认这些担忧，他在脚注中写道，法院决定采取“额外措施来保护集体利益”。 马丁内斯-奥尔金表示，一旦支出最终确定，律师将被要求提交分配后会计报告，如果他们的费用减少，那么法院“可能会减少”费用。

尽管和解可能让三位作者松了一口气，他们花了数年时间捍卫自己的作品和潜在集体成员的 506,194 件作品，但主要原告可能会对将他们请求的服务奖从 50,000 美元减少到 15,000 美元的决定感到失望。

但马丁内斯-奥尔金表示，尽管作者在诉讼和和解谈判上花费了大量时间和资源，但较高的金额“不合理”。 由于没有迹象表明作者可能因提起诉讼而面临报复，法官裁定应给予较低的赔偿。

主要原告在给路透社的一份声明中表示，和解协议“让我们离 Anthropic 的真正责任又近了一步，并让所有人工智能公司注意到，他们不能逃避法律或凌驾于创作者的权利之上。”

作者每件作品获得超过 3,000 美元的收入虽然不太可能，但仍有可能。

“如果在支付所有有效索赔后和解基金中仍有任何资金，双方预计会将剩余资金重新分配给和解集体成员，除非这样做在经济上不可行，”马丁内斯-奥尔金说。

作者的律师对法官的批准表示庆祝，并在一份新闻稿中写道，“版权持有者聚集在一起对 Anthropic 的盗版行为表示不满”，并称他们对和解的支持“压倒性”。

人类似乎也很高兴结束了关于和解的争论。 Anthropic 的副总法律顾问 Aparna Sridhar 在向 Ars 提供的一份声明中表示，Anthropic 很高兴该案件具有里程碑意义的裁决证明其人工智能培训属于合理使用，并且很高兴大多数权利持有人已经提出索赔。

斯里达尔表示：“我们很高兴和解协议涵盖的超过 91% 的作者和出版商都要求支付他们应得的份额，我们期待着解决这个问题。”

人类阻止了最后一刻的选择退出

对于每一次批准的选择退出，Anthropic 都面临着单独诉讼的风险，马丁内斯-奥尔金表示，这仍然是一个需要法院评估的复杂案件。 那么，Anthropic 的动机是阻止延迟的选择退出请求，这是有道理的。

一些反对和解的作者辩称，他们没有及时收到通知，选择退出期限太短，而且对作者的和解裁决是不可接受的，因为《版权法》允许潜在的更高的法定损害赔偿。

Martínez-Olguín 证实，有 350 名集体成员成功选择退出和解，但数十项反对意见被驳回，至少 9 位作者在 3 月 30 日截止日期后直接提出选择退出的请求也被拒绝。

最终，马丁内斯-奥尔金驳回了大部分反对意见，并拒绝了大部分迟来的选择退出请求。

马丁内斯-奥尔金说，法院只接受了两位作者提出的迟到的选择退出请求，这些作者因未能在截止日期前完成而表现出“可原谅的疏忽”。

两人是共同作者，但没有收到和解通知。 一位作者晚几天提出了选择退出请求，该请求获得批准，而另一位作者由于中风，在更长时间的延迟后才获得批准。 该作者还指出，她“住在墨西哥，说西班牙语，无法理解集体通知，并声称没有提供西班牙语翻译”，从而赢得了法院的同情。

最后一刻试图逃避和解的作者之一是唐纳德·帕斯曼 (Donald Passman)，他是一位娱乐律师兼作家，他于 6 月底发出了请求。 那是在截止日期之后三个月，马丁内斯-奥尔金说这是“不可原谅的迟到”，因为记录显示通知已发送到他目前的地址，并且至少“收到了一份通知”，尽管比选择退出截止日期晚了一个多月。

帕斯曼在一份法庭文件中辩称，证据显示他收到了较晚的和解通知。 据称，他甚至无法依赖出版商西蒙与舒斯特（Simon & Schuster）转发通知，并且“不知道是否存在任何选择退出的截止日期”，直到他在截止日期之后收到一封信，只邀请他领取付款，而没有提及选择退出。

帕斯曼没有立即回应 Ars 就和解批准发表评论的请求。 但他可能会感到失望，因为他认为他的书《关于音乐行业你需要知道的一切》是一本重要的行业文本，“每件作品的和解费用预计约为 3,351.39 美元，不足以补偿我个人版权索赔的潜在价值。”

Anthropic 反对帕斯曼选择退出的企图，称他在得知和解协议后花了几个月的时间才提出退出请求。 据 Anthropic 称，据称帕斯曼“不知何故没有听说在这起引起大量头条新闻的案件中达成有史以来最大的版权和解协议”，这令人难以置信。

“在如此长时间且不合理的拖延之后，允许帕斯曼先生选择退出，将导致其他人也做出同样的尝试，从而破坏最终结果，并冒着和解资金分配不当延迟的风险，”Anthropic 辩称。 “在数十万人的班级中，只有一小部分（大约 400 人）试图选择退出。”

马丁内斯-奥尔金同意 Anthropic 的观点，并表示同意帕斯曼的请求可能会导致其他作者退出和解。

马丁内斯·奥尔金在命令中提醒作者，和解协议提供了他们似乎忽视的非金钱利益。 首先，作者可以避免昂贵、漫长、复杂的诉讼，这些诉讼可能导致“零赔偿”的损失，她说，并援引先例表明“迅速结案”为集体成员提供了价值。 另一方面，和解协议要求 Anthropic 销毁他们的所有作品，并允许在尘埃落定后，如果 Anthropic 滥用他们的作品，可以在未来提起诉讼。

阿什利 (Ashley) 是 Ars Technica 的高级政策记者，致力于追踪新兴政策和新技术的社会影响。 她是一位拥有 20 年经验的芝加哥记者。

54 条评论
